projectDescription: A community-driven, open-source vertical scrolling rhythm game (VSRG) built on osu!framework, featuring a built-in map editor, Lua-powered storyboarding, custom skinning, multiplayer, and cross-game map imports.
keyFeatures: Vertical scrolling rhythm gameplay with 1–10+ key mode support
Built-in map editor with charting, design, and storyboard tabs
Lua-powered storyboarding and skin scripting system
Custom JSON-based skin format with per-keymode configuration
Game modifier system (rate changes, difficulty adjustments, automation)
Multiplayer and online leaderboards via the fluXis API
Map importers for osu!, Quaver, and Stepmania formats
Plugin architecture for extending importer and settings functionality
Steam release with Desktop and Android build targets
publicApiSurface: MapInfo — JSON map definition (HitObjects, TimingPoints, ScrollVelocities, MapEvents)
HitObject — Note structure (time, lane, holdTime, type: Normal/Tick/Landmine)
MapEvents — Flash, ColorFade, LaneSwitch, Shader, PlayfieldMove, BeatPulse, etc.
SkinJson — Skin configuration (per-keymode column width, hit position, colors, judgement colors)
SkinKeymode — Column width, hit position, tint flags, lane colors per key count
IMod interface — Name, Acronym, ScoreMultiplier, Rankable, IncompatibleMods
HitWindows — Judgement timing windows (Flawless / Perfect / Great / Alright / Okay / Miss)
Plugin abstract class — Name, Author, Version, CreateImporter(), CreateSettings()
ScriptRunner — Lua scripting engine: AddFunction, AddField, Run, DefineParameter
Storyboard Lua API — Add(), StoryboardBox/Sprite/Text/Circle constructors, screen, metadata, settings globals
Shared Lua globals — SetVersion, RandomRange, BPMAtTime, DefineParameter, print
FluXisSetting enum — Full list of configurable game settings keys
